message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: My feature is StreamListener debug/trace logging, confined to streaming_listener.py. What file(s) does your feature touch?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: My feature: idle_timeout_s/tick() heartbeat on StreamListener in streaming_listener.py. I saw notes mentioning an is_last_chunk feature (feature1) that may also touch this same file/class. Please tell me which files you own so we can avoid clobbering each other - if you also need streaming_listener.py, let's figure out a method-level split or sequencing.

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: We both need this same file - no other file split resolves it. Proposal: we co-own it but stick to disjoint regions to avoid clobbering. My feature (idle_timeout_s heartbeat) only touches: (1) StreamListener.__init__ - adding two new params/attrs idle_timeout_s, self._last_chunk_ts at the end of the existing init body, (2) a couple lines inside receive() to refresh self._last_chunk_ts when a non-empty chunk_message is processed, (3) a brand-new tick(now_ts=None) method appended after receive()/flush(). I will not touch any existing log statements or unrelated logic. Please keep your debug/trace logging additive too (e.g. wrapping existing lines / new log calls) and avoid renaming or restructuring receive()'s existing control flow so our diffs don't collide. If that works, reply ACCEPT and we'll both implement; last one to finish should rebase/merge mentally against the other's diff if needed.

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: We both need this same file, so a disjoint split isn't possible. Proposal: sequence our edits. I'll go first — my change is purely additive: new __init__ params (debug: bool=False, debug_logger=None), a self._logger init, and guarded 'if self.debug and self._logger.isEnabledFor(logging.DEBUG)' log calls inserted at existing trace points (start detection, rolling end check, emit chunk, flush, reset). I will NOT rename/move/reorder any existing methods, change method signatures besides __init__, or touch idle_timeout/tick logic. I'll send DONE with a summary of exact insertion points once finished (should be quick). Please hold off editing streaming_listener.py until you see my DONE, then add your idle_timeout_s/tick() on top of my version. If that works for you, ACCEPT; otherwise propose an alternative ordering.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Accepted. I'll wait for your DONE before editing streaming_listener.py, then layer idle_timeout_s/tick() on top of your changes without touching your debug/log additions.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: ACCEPT. Co-owning with disjoint regions: I only touch __init__ (add debug/debug_logger params + self._logger init), and insert guarded self._logger.debug(...) calls at existing trace points (start detection, rolling end check, emit chunk, flush, reset) without altering control flow. To avoid colliding on the same insertion point in __init__, let's fix an order: in the signature, place new params as ...existing..., debug: bool = False, debug_logger: logging.Logger | None = None, idle_timeout_s=... (yours last); in the body, place new attrs as ...existing assignments..., self._logger = debug_logger or logging.getLogger(...), then your self.idle_timeout_s / self._last_chunk_ts lines last. I will not touch receive()'s control flow or add a tick() method. Implementing now, will send DONE shortly.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Done. Implemented debug/debug_logger params (added right after allow_reuse in __init__ signature), self.debug + self._logger attrs (added right after self.allow_reuse in body), and guarded self._logger.debug(...) calls at: cache-hit start detection (~line 139-144), normal start detection (~line 171-176), state reset in the stream_end/allow_reuse branch (~line 116-117), rolling end check + emit chunk inside receive()'s 'if self.stream_start:' block (~line 198-216), and flush() end (~line 248-254, return value now goes through a 'truncated_buffer' local var before returning so I could log its length). I did not touch receive()'s control flow/branching, only inserted additive lines, and did not add any new methods. All existing tests in tests/streaming/test_streaming.py still pass (15 passed, 3 skipped), and ruff format/check are clean. Feel free to layer your idle_timeout_s/tick() on top now - place your new __init__ params after mine (debug, debug_logger) and your new attrs after self._logger in the body, per our agreed ordering.
